task_completion.visual_evidence: SolveSpace application window is open displaying the 2D/3D sketch of the 'side' part.
The Property Browser in SolveSpace shows an extrusion group with a depth/thickness value of 5.0 mm, or the 3D view clearly shows the extrusion operation being applied.
A 'Save As' dialog showing the filename 'side_extruded.slvs' in the '~/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory, or the SolveSpace window title updated to 'side_extruded.slvs'.

task_completion.description: Determine the volume of the part in mm³
task_completion.visual_evidence: SolveSpace's 'Analyze' -> 'Volume' or 'Measure' dialog/text is visible on screen, displaying the calculated volume of the solid in mm³.
A text editor or terminal window showing the file 'mass_report.txt' containing the final calculated mass value in grams.

integrity.description: Agent used the SolveSpace GUI to perform the extrusion and measure the volume
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with SolveSpace menus, property browser, and analyze tools, rather than just running a script or editing files blindly.

integrity.description: The mass reported is genuinely calculated from the volume found in SolveSpace
integrity.visual_evidence: The mass value in 'mass_report.txt' should mathematically match the volume shown on-screen in SolveSpace multiplied by 0.0027. It should not be a random or hallucinated number.

integrity.description: The extruded model was saved properly through the CAD software
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used SolveSpace's 'File -> Save As' functionality to create 'side_extruded.slvs', rather than just copying the file or creating an empty text file with a .slvs extension.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original file 'side.slvs' is already a 3D solid (~5.99 mm thick, volume ~155405 mm³). Adding a new 5.0 mm extrusion unions with the existing solid, keeping the volume at ~155405 mm³ (mass ~419.6 g). If the agent modifies the existing extrusion to 5.0 mm instead, the volume becomes ~129625 mm³ (mass ~350.0 g). You must pass the mass calculation step if the value in 'mass_report.txt' equals the onscreen volume shown in SolveSpace multiplied by 0.0027 g/mm³, regardless of which of the two volumes the agent ended up with.
